reasoning: The bearish consensus rests on structural technicals (CLEAN_BEAR ribbon, price 138¢ below pivot) and macro backdrop (gap down -3.17, VIX +7.54%), but premarket price action is defending the overnight gap, not confirming capitulation. Price has held a $0.42 tight range (706.71-707.13) for 1+ hour with the high held at 707.13 — active buyer defense is visible. The bearish agents assume 706.71 (premarket low) will be tested downward as inevitable; however, this battle-level test hasn't occurred yet, and price is still $0.17 above it. Meanwhile, energy is outperforming (+1.12% vs SPY +0.57%), which could signal early risk-on rotation rather than the 'defensive' interpretation offered. VIX at 18.83 is MID-regime, not extreme — the macro agent itself notes the absolute level is not alarming, only the +7.54% overnight move. Historical gap-and-rip patterns (overnight panic + tight premarket range + zero macro relief events + no earnings today = no intraday volatility catalyst) suggest intraday recovery is high-probability. The consensus assumes technicals drive price; price action suggests buyers have structural edge TODAY.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: If SPY opens at or above 707.13 and holds that level (premarket high) for 15+ minutes into RTH, breaking the implied resistance
invalidation_scenarios.effect: This invalidates the CLEAN_BEAR ribbon narrative — price would have reclaimed resistance and showed buyer control. Bearish thesis predicts price retreats to test 706.71 support; instead, it advances. Opens door to 708.0 and higher.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: If XLE continues outperformance and widens to +1.8% vs SPY (current +0.57% gap only at +1.12% for energy) by 09:45 ET
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Energy momentum acceleration would suggest commodity-driven risk-on rotation, not risk-off. Macro backdrop would flip from 'fear building' to 'inflation narrative.' Sector divergence would contradict the unified bearish consensus.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: If VIX falls below 18.00 (drop of 0.83 points) by 10:00 ET — within 4 hours of premarket open
invalidation_scenarios.effect: VIX rising +7.54% is the macro agent's keystone signal for 'building fear.' A reversion to sub-18.00 within morning hours would prove the overnight spike was transient noise, not a macro regime shift. This deflates the 'risk-off' consensus entirely.

weakest_consensus_link: All agents assume battle-level 706.71 will be tested downward as inevitable, but price has been above 706.71 the entire premarket (only $0.17 away, never tested). The tight range suggests consolidation + buyer defense, not downside conviction. Agents are planning around a scenario that hasn't been confirmed by price action yet.
key_observations: Premarket defense of overnight gap: price held a $0.42 range for 1+ hour with high at 707.13 — this is active buying, not capitulation
Energy outperformance (+1.12%) could be early-warning of risk-on rotation, not a one-off defensive bounce
VIX in MID regime at 18.83 is moderate, not extreme — macro agent admits absolute level is not alarming
Gap-and-rip pattern: overnight panic (-3.17) + tight premarket range + zero macro catalysts = classic intraday recovery setup historically
Narrow breadth based on 3 sectors only; tech/healthcare/industrials/materials not sampled — hidden strength possible
Internals agent only 0.40 confidence (near-neutral); lowest conviction in the consensus
Level agent confidence 0.65 (lowest structural signal confidence); scenario mapping assumes downside test but price hasn't confirmed it yet
